description: Letter dealing with a legal matter that was discussed before a qadi concerning the consequences of having had Jewish witnesses. (Information from Goitein's index cards.) Letter from the 13th century. An angry trader of gems reports that he was forced to provide Muslim witnesses for a Muslim court in Fustat and that his Jewish witnesses were not accepted. He mentions a restriction of R. Yiṣḥaq b. Ḥalfon from Alexandria that one should not make exception to 'gentile manners'. Trade in Lazuward (Lapis lazuli) is also mentioned. (Information from Frenkel. See also Goitein, Med. Soc. 4:208 no. 411)
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בבית המדרש לרבנים.
